pi_items.source: Found in task description (line 4), export_result.sh line 12, and setup_task.sh line 13. Task explicitly states: 'Rename and save the downloaded file to /home/ga/Documents/last_month_sales.csv'

WooCommerce official documentation and code analysis shows Sales by Date reports include Date, order counts, and sales figures. Exact CSV header format not explicitly documented in official WooCommerce sources. Verifier.py (line 74) flexibly checks for either exact string or presence of 'Date' and 'Gross Sales' keywords, suggesting format may vary by WooCommerce version.

setup_task.sh lines 50-74 explicitly creates 5 backdated orders spread across the previous month (LAST_MONTH_START to LAST_MONTH_END). Each order has line items and pricing. Export will contain at minimum 5 rows of data, satisfying min_data_rows requirement.

privileged_info_summary: This task exports WooCommerce Sales by Date report data. The output file path is definitively /home/ga/Documents/last_month_sales.csv. The task setup creates 5 synthetic backdated orders distributed across the previous calendar month using bash date arithmetic to calculate date ranges, then uses WP-CLI and SQL to backdate post and metadata timestamps. The CSV will contain at least 5 data rows (potentially aggregated by date). The exact CSV header format (Date,Orders,Gross Sales) cannot be independently verified as WooCommerce official documentation does not publicly specify this legacy Reports export format; the verifier allows flexible matching of header names.
